Package org.example.service

Class SessionManager

java.lang.Object
org.example.service.SessionManager
public class SessionManager extends Object
Stores login session details for the current user.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    SessionManager(AuthEventLogger authEventLogger, EventManager eventManager)
    Creates a new session manager object with the given values.

    Logs in a default admin session used by compatibility flows.

    Logs in a user email with the default USER role.

    Logs in a user with a specific role.

    Logs in using an existing user object.

    Clears all session values and logs out the current user.

  • Constructor Details

    • SessionManager

      public SessionManager(AuthEventLogger authEventLogger, EventManager eventManager)
      Creates a new session manager object with the given values.
      Parameters:
      authEventLogger - value for auth event logger
      eventManager - manager object used for shared app state

  • Method Details

    • login

      public void login(String email)
      Logs in a user email with the default USER role.
      Parameters:
      email - email address used for login or matching

    • login

      public void login(String email, UserRole role)
      Logs in a user with a specific role.
      Parameters:
      email - email address used for login or matching
      role - role value used for access control

    • login

      public void login(SystemUser user)
      Logs in using an existing user object.
      Parameters:
      user - user involved in this action

    • login

      public void login()
      Logs in a default admin session used by compatibility flows.

    • logout

      public void logout()
      Clears all session values and logs out the current user.

    • logoutAndNotify

      public void logoutAndNotify()
      Logs out and sends a logout event message to observers.

    • isLoggedIn

      public boolean isLoggedIn()
      Checks whether logged in is true.
      Returns:
      true when the action is valid or successful, otherwise false
